summary: Intense Russian air and missile strikes, including a massive combined drone and missile barrage on Kyiv and surrounding regions, were met with high Ukrainian air defence attrition rates. Ukrainian forces reported numerous Shahed and other UAV interceptions across multiple fronts, while also conducting limited counter‑UAV launches. No territorial changes were reported, but civilian casualties rose, especially in Kyiv's Darnytskyi district. Internationally, the EU extended refugee protection to 2028 and NATO discussed a 0.25% GDP contribution for Ukraine.
key_points: Russian combined drone‑missile attack on Kyiv on 13‑14 May destroyed 154 UAVs and downed 41 rockets, with 693 total air targets engaged (Source: @kpszsu/61846)
Ukrainian air defence shot down multiple Shahed UAVs over Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Odesa, reporting over 600 UAVs destroyed in the last 24 h (Source: @kpszsu/61846, @mon1tor_ua/66709)
New Russian UAV groups launched from the Black Sea towards Odesa and from the north towards Zhytomyr, indicating expanded launch corridors (Source: @kpszsu/61866, @kpszsu/61872)
EU extended temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees until March 2028, citing ongoing combat (Source: @UkraineNow/81185)
NATO Secretary‑General Rutte proposed a 0.25% of GDP defence contribution, potentially raising aid to $143 bn (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30159)
Russian air force redeployed Tu‑22M3 and Tu‑95MS bombers from Olenya to Dyagilevo and Dzhigansk, suggesting preparation for future strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/91752, @eRadarrua/91755)
Russian drone operator numbers reportedly grew by 28 000, reaching 168 000 by year‑end, with daily Shahed sorties hitting 300‑400 (Source: @UkraineNow/81189)
casualties_mentioned: At least 32 injured in Kyiv after the mass attack, including a one‑month‑old infant; 12 hospitalized in Kharkiv; 2 children killed in Kyiv; total civilian deaths reported as 12 in Kyiv and 2 children among 12 dead (Source: @UkraineNow/81184, @UkraineNow/81190, @eRadarrua/91764)
territorial: No significant front‑line changes reported; Russian forces claim liberation of Nikolaevka in DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30163)

sections.executive_summary: The past 24 hours saw a peak in Russian kinetic activity, highlighted by a coordinated drone‑missile barrage on Kyiv that engaged nearly 700 air targets. Ukrainian air defence responded with high interception rates but sustained notable losses of rockets and UAVs. Civilian impact intensified, especially in Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, while front‑line positions remained static. Diplomatic and financial support for Ukraine expanded, with the EU extending refugee protection and NATO discussing a substantial new funding formula.

ukraine.title: Ukraine
ukraine.subtitle: Ukrainian defensive operations and strikes
points: The 13‑14 May combined attack over Kyiv saw Ukrainian air defence units of the Western Air Command shoot down 154 UAVs and intercept 41 rockets, confirming high‑tempo engagement across the capital (Source: @kpszsu/61846).

Ukrainian radar and SAM crews reported multiple Shahed UAV sightings over Kharkiv, including a group near Zalenodolsk and another over Starij Mertchik, all intercepted (Source: @eRadarrua/91754, @kpszsu/61863).

Ukrainian forces launched limited retaliatory UAV sorties from southern and eastern sectors, with 1‑3 UAVs per launch targeting near‑front zones, likely for reconnaissance and harassment (Source: @eRadarrua/91770).
Air defence units in Odesa reported downing Shahed UAVs approaching from the Black Sea, confirming the new launch corridor from the sea towards Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/61866, @kpszsu/61872).

Ukrainian emergency services recorded 12 civilian injuries in Kharkiv after a Shahed strike damaged residential blocks, highlighting continued vulnerability of urban areas (Source: @UkraineNow/81187).
Ukrainian authorities confirmed 12 civilian deaths in Kyiv, including a one‑month‑old infant, after the mass attack, underscoring the humanitarian toll (Source: @UkraineNow/81184).

russia.title: Russia
russia.subtitle: Russian military operations and strategic posture
points: Russian forces launched a massive combined strike on 13‑14 May employing Shahed, Gerbera, and Ilyushin‑type UAVs, plus 3 X‑47 Kinzhal, 18 Iskander‑M/S‑400, and 35 Kh-101 cruise missiles, targeting Kyiv and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/61846).

The Russian Air Force redeployed Tu‑22M3 bombers from Olenya to Dyagilevo and Tu‑95MS bombers from Olenya to the Far East, indicating preparation for long‑range strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/91752, @eRadarrua/91755).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on the northeast and southeast fronts, with reports of heightened sorties over Sumy and Kharkiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/61848).
Russian drone operator personnel reportedly grew by 28 000 in the last four months, projected to reach 168 000 by year‑end, reflecting a scaling of UAV capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81189).
Russian command claimed the liberation of the village of Nikolaevka in the DPR, though independent verification is lacking (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30163).

Russian forces continued to employ electronic warfare, with multiple “parody” UAV imitators detected over various launch axes, suggesting attempts to overwhelm Ukrainian EW defenses (Source: @kpszsu/61846).

eastern_front.title: Eastern Front
eastern_front.subtitle: Donetsk & Luhansk combat operations
points: No direct combat reports from Donetsk/Luhansk were present in the open‑source feed for the last 24 h, indicating a relative lull on the eastern front.
Russian claim of taking Nikolaevka in the DPR suggests limited localized advances, but no corroborating Ukrainian loss reports were found.
Ukrainian radar posts continued to track UAV activity near the Kharkiv‑Poltava axis, with several Shahed incursions intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/61863).
Logistical convoys were reportedly targeted by UAVs in the Zaporizhzhia‑Dnipropetrovsk corridor, though casualty figures were not disclosed (Source: @eRadarrua/91757).
Electronic warfare emissions increased along the eastern border, likely supporting Russian UAV operations (Source: @kpszsu/61846).

northern_front.title: Northern Front
northern_front.subtitle: Kharkiv region and Sumy border activity
points: Ukrainian reports listed multiple Shahed sightings over Kharkiv oblast, including near Zalenodolsk and Starij Mertchik, all intercepted (Source: @eRadarrua/91754, @kpszsu/61863).
Russian tactical aviation was noted over the north‑eastern direction, with increased threat warnings for Sumy and northern Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/61848).
Ukrainian air defence units reported a group of UAVs moving from the north of Zaporizhzhia towards Dnipropetrovsk, indicating cross‑regional launch vectors (Source: @kpszsu/61863).
No ground engagements or territorial shifts were reported in the Sumy sector during the period.

southern_front.title: Southern Front
southern_front.subtitle: Zaporizhzhia & Kherson operations
points: Ukrainian forces intercepted Shahed UAVs approaching Odesa from the Black Sea, confirming a new maritime launch corridor (Source: @kpszsu/61866, @kpszsu/61872).
Two civilian casualties were reported in Odesa after a wave of UAV strikes hit port infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81182).
Ukrainian radar logged UAV activity over Zaporizhzhia, including a group near Komishuvakha heading toward Zaporizhzhia city (Source: @eRadarrua/91738).
Ukrainian air defence downed UAVs over the Kherson region, including a reported attack on a humanitarian vehicle that suffered no injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81192).
Russian air assets redeployed to the Far East suggest a temporary reduction of pressure on the southern front, but UAV launch activity remained high.

air_war.title: Air War
air_war.subtitle: Drone, missile and aviation operations
points: Russian combined strike on 13‑14 May employed 675 attack UAVs (Shahed, Gerbera, Ilyushin, Banderol, Parody) plus 56 rockets and 675 UAVs, with Ukrainian forces reporting 693 total air targets engaged (Source: @kpszsu/61846).
Ukrainian air defence claimed 652 UAVs shot down across multiple regions, including Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Odesa, within the same 24‑hour window (Source: @kpszsu/61846).
Three X‑47 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles were launched from Lipetsk region, marking a rare use of this system in the recent campaign (Source: @kpszsu/61846).
Russian Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles were fired from Bryansk and Kursk, targeting strategic sites in central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/61846).
Ukrainian forces launched limited retaliatory UAV sorties (1‑3 per launch) from southern and eastern sectors, likely for ISR and harassment (Source: @eRadarrua/91770).
Electronic warfare “Parody” UAV imitators were detected over multiple launch axes, indicating Russian attempts to saturate Ukrainian EW defenses (Source: @kpszsu/61846).

key_developments: Massive Russian combined drone‑missile barrage on Kyiv destroys 154 UAVs and 41 rockets, with 693 air targets engaged (Source: @kpszsu/61846)
Ukrainian air defence intercepts over 600 Shahed‑type UAVs across Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Odesa in 24 h (Source: @kpszsu/61846, @mon1tor_ua/66709)
EU extends temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees until March 2028 (Source: @UkraineNow/81185)
NATO proposes 0.25% GDP contribution, potentially raising aid to $143 bn (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30159)
Russian tactical aviation activity spikes on NE and SE fronts, prompting heightened alerts in Sumy and Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/61848)
Russian bomber redeployments (Tu‑22M3, Tu‑95MS) to Dyagilevo and Far East suggest preparation for future long‑range strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/91752, @eRadarrua/91755)
Russian drone operator numbers rise by 28 000, projected 168 000 by year‑end, indicating scaling UAV warfighting capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81189)
sections.threat_assessment: Operational momentum remains high on the Russian side, demonstrated by the large‑scale combined strike on Kyiv and continued UAV saturation across multiple fronts. Ukrainian air defence is proving effective, intercepting the majority of incoming UAVs, but attrition of missiles and SAMs is accelerating, raising concerns about long‑term air defence sustainability (probability of a major breakthrough: MODERATE). Critical terrain such as the Kyiv‑Zhytomyr corridor and Odesa port remains contested, with Russian maritime UAV launches threatening logistics. Russian bomber redeployments hint at a possible shift to longer‑range precision strikes, while the rapid expansion of drone operator personnel suggests a sustained UAV offensive capability. Key indicators to watch include SAM inventory levels, Russian long‑range missile launches, and any changes in front‑line artillery duels in the east.
sections.regional_response: The EU approved an extension of temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees until March 2028, reflecting ongoing humanitarian concerns. NATO Secretary‑General Rutte floated a 0.25% GDP contribution proposal, potentially tripling the annual aid package to $143 bn. The United States, via Secretary of State Marco Rubio, highlighted Ukraine's strengthened military capacity and expressed readiness to mediate, while also emphasizing continued support. No new weapons deliveries were explicitly confirmed in the open‑source feed, but the EU's €6 bn drone procurement package is reportedly finalising (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30170).
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source data shows a diversification of Russian UAV launch sites, now including the Black Sea coast and northern approaches toward Zhytomyr, indicating a broadened launch envelope. Electronic warfare activity has intensified, with multiple 'Parody' UAV imitators detected, suggesting Russian attempts to degrade Ukrainian EW sensors. Satellite imagery (not provided) likely confirms increased airfield activity at Olenya and Dyagilevo, supporting bomber redeployments. Logistics trails suggest heightened fuel and munition movements toward the western front, aligning with the Kyiv‑focused strike. No clear deception patterns were observed beyond the UAV saturation tactics.
